title: Implement Journal Driver De-duplication
problem: The journaling pipeline currently lists identical tweets from the same source multiple times as separate drivers for a belief axis if they are processed within the same daily cycle. This inflates the perceived number of distinct drivers and can misrepresent the diversity or strength of evidence influencing a belief.
evidence: From the 2026-04-14 Daily Report, the 'Digital Surveillance and Individual Autonomy' axis was driven by three entries: 'Concerns about Amazon drone delivery mapping neighborhoods and tracking routines highlight issues of [@JasonBassler1/status/2043828578375348234]', 'Concerns are raised about privacy and surveillance implications of drone delivery, highlighting pote [@JasonBassler1/status/2043828578375348234]', and 'Concerns are raised about Amazon drone delivery mapping neighborhoods, tracking routines, and normal [@JasonBassler1/status/2043828578375348234]'. These three entries are identical in source and content, yet are listed as distinct drivers.
proposed_solution: Modify the journaling pipeline to identify and consolidate identical tweets originating from the same user within a single daily journaling cycle. Instead of creating multiple driver entries for the same piece of information, the pipeline should aggregate these into a single driver entry. This entry could include a 'repetition count' or similar metadata to indicate how many times the identical content was encountered, thereby providing a more accurate representation of the unique informational inputs.
resolution: Pipeline error: Tests failed: Blocked test command (unsafe): # This change introduces library files, not a directly executable script.; Blocked test command (unsafe): # To test, a new test script would be needed, for example:; Blocked test command (unsafe): # node tests/test_journal_deduplication.js
